name: Twelve Data Financial Markets API
what_it_does: Financial markets data API providing real-time and historical stock, forex, crypto, and ETF data with built-in technical indicators (80+), WebSocket streaming, and supports both REST and WebSocket connections.
use_cases: Real-time stock and forex price feeds for algorithmic trading agents
Computing technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands) server-side via API
Historical OHLCV data retrieval for backtesting and ML model training
Crypto market data aggregation across major exchanges from single API
Building financial screeners and alerts on price movements
not_for: Level 2 order book data and trade flow analysis (use Bloomberg or Refinitiv)
Fundamental financial data (earnings, balance sheets) without supplementation
Institutional-grade tick data at microsecond resolution
best_when: An agent needs affordable real-time and historical market data with built-in technical indicators for retail trading automation or financial analysis.
avoid_when: You need institutional data quality, L2 order books, or alternative data — use Bloomberg or Refinitiv.

auth.notes: API key from Twelve Data account. Passed as query parameter or header. Single key per account with usage-based rate limits. Separate keys not required for different endpoints.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 800 API calls/day, 8 symbols in real-time, daily data only
pricing.paid_tiers: Grow from $29/month (55k calls/day, minute data); Pro from $79/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development and basic use. Paid plans unlock more calls, intraday data, WebSocket, and additional symbols. Competitive pricing vs Bloomberg/Refinitiv at retail level.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 8 requests/minute; paid: scales with plan (55+ calls/minute)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive REST API documentation. 80+ built-in technical indicators documented with formulas. Error responses include error codes and human-readable messages. WebSocket documentation for real-time feeds.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All data requests are read-only and idempotent. Same query returns same result for same timestamp range.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are strict on free tier — 8 requests/minute caps burst usage for agents
Real-time data requires paid plan — free tier only provides EOD (end-of-day) data
WebSocket reconnection must be handled by agent — no built-in reconnect guarantee
Exchange coverage varies by asset class — verify symbol availability before building workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key passed as query parameter (less secure than header — could appear in logs). No scope granularity beyond read-only access. Suitable for retail financial data — not regulated financial infrastructure.
